headline: Legal recognition of customary marriages remains inconsistent
context: Customary marriages among indigenous and non-Muslim communities face inconsistent legal recognition across states, affecting property and inheritance rights.

cards.big: Customary marriages in 8 indigenous communities lack consistent legal recognition
cards.sub: Property and inheritance rights depend on which state the couple resides in

cards.big: Law Reform (Marriage and Divorce) Act 1976 does not cover customary marriages
cards.sub: Aboriginal Peoples Act 1954 silent on marriage recognition and rights

cards.big: CEDAW Article 16 requires equal rights in marriage regardless of ceremony type
cards.sub: Women in unregistered customary marriages have no statutory property rights

cards.big: Estimated 12,000 customary marriages unregistered across Peninsular Malaysia
cards.sub: Children of unregistered marriages face documentation and inheritance challenges

cards.big: 12,000 families denied legal protection because their traditions are unrecognised
NRD launched voluntary customary marriage registration pilot in Pahang in 2024
